schools.school_name: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. School

level.label: Level
level.desc: Grade band served: elementary, k8, middle, high, alternative.

is_closure_candidate.label: Low enrollment (WW)
is_closure_candidate.desc: One of the 15 lowest-enrollment schools listed by Willamette Week (2026-03-18). Often discussed as a closure shortlist, but PPS has not published its own list; that is expected Nov 2026, with a board vote in Dec 2026.

closure_rank.label: WW low-enroll. rank
closure_rank.desc: Willamette Week's enrollment-based rank within the bottom 15 (1 = smallest). Not a PPS rank or prediction.

functional_capacity_2021.label: Functional capacity
functional_capacity_2021.desc: PPS's planning number for how many students a building can educate: gross capacity (classrooms × student-station size by area) minus set-asides (SPED focus, art/music, computer lab, DLI co-location) and reduced by a configuration-specific utilization rate (85% at middle/high, higher at K-5/K-8), with further reductions for Title I and TSI/CSI schools. From the 2021 Long-Range Facility Plan.

utilization_pct_2526.label: Utilization 25-26
utilization_pct_2526.desc: 2025-26 enrollment ÷ 2021 functional capacity. Below 50% is substantially underused; above 100% is overcrowded per PPS's own planning standard.

year_built.label: Year built
year_built.desc: Year the main building was constructed.

square_feet.label: Square feet
square_feet.desc: Building square footage (2009 inventory; may predate recent bond expansions).

construction_type_2009.label: Construction
construction_type_2009.desc: Structural type: URM (unreinforced masonry), LRCW (reinforced concrete wall), Wood, Concrete, Steel, Masonry.

is_urm_building.label: URM?
is_urm_building.desc: Unreinforced masonry: highest seismic risk category.

urm_retrofit_cost_usd.label: URM retrofit $
urm_retrofit_cost_usd.desc: Estimated cost of a partial retrofit targeting just the unreinforced-masonry areas (URM schools only). Meant as a lower-cost option vs. full-building retrofit.

retrofit_cost_remaining_usd.label: Retrofit cost remaining
retrofit_cost_remaining_usd.desc: Holmes 2024 rough-order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost to fully retrofit every building on this campus. Schools shown as $0 were excluded from Holmes's scope due to recent or near-complete modernization (Benson, Cleveland, Franklin, Grant, Jefferson, Kellogg, Lent, Lewis, Lincoln, McDaniel, Roosevelt, Rosa Parks, Faubion, Ida B Wells, Creative Science/Clark).

seismic_retrofit_status.label: Seismic retrofit
seismic_retrofit_status.desc: 'full' = full modernization, 'targeted' = roof/partial retrofit done, 'planned_*' = 2025 bond scheduled, blank = none.

recent_boundary_change.label: Recent boundary change
recent_boundary_change.desc: Schools whose attendance boundary or grade configuration was meaningfully redrawn inside the 2018→2025 window. 'DBRAC 2018' = affected by the Districtwide Boundary Review (new middle-school feeders, K-8→K-5 conversions, NE/N redraws). 'SEGC 2023' = affected by the Southeast Guiding Coalition (Harrison Park and Bridger reconfigured, Lent/Marysville/Clark feeder redraws). Raw enrollment trends for these rows partly reflect catchment redraws, not pure demand change.
recent_boundary_change.source: Curated from PPS DBRAC + SEGC implementation documentation

is_title_i.label: Title I?
is_title_i.desc: Receives federal Title I-A schoolwide funding (high-poverty designation).

pct_regular_attenders_2425.label: % Regular attenders 24-25
pct_regular_attenders_2425.desc: Share of students who attended more than 90% of their enrolled days in 2024-25. Oregon's core attendance metric (students not chronically absent). Statewide average was 66.5% in 2024-25.

pct_experienced_teachers_2425.label: % Experienced teachers 24-25
pct_experienced_teachers_2425.desc: Share of teachers at this school with 3+ years of experience in education (any district). A higher value usually means a more stable, veteran faculty.

pct_teacher_retention_2425.label: % Teacher retention 24-25
pct_teacher_retention_2425.desc: Share of teachers who returned from the previous school year; a turnover indicator. Low retention often reflects instability or chronic understaffing.

class_size_2425.label: Median class size 24-25
class_size_2425.desc: ODE-reported median class size (all self-contained classes in 2024-25). Oregon statewide median is 24.

pct_ela_prof_2425.label: % ELA prof 24-25
pct_ela_prof_2425.desc: Percent of students meeting/exceeding on Oregon ELA state test (all grades, all students).

pct_math_prof_2425.label: % Math prof 24-25
pct_math_prof_2425.desc: Percent of students meeting/exceeding on Oregon Math state test (all grades, all students).

crdc_chronic_absent_2020.label: Chronic absent (CRDC)
crdc_chronic_absent_2020.desc: Count of students chronically absent (missed ≥15 days). Based on 2020 COVID year; likely suppressed.

crdc_idea_2020.label: IDEA (SPED) students
crdc_idea_2020.desc: Students on IEPs under IDEA (special education).

frl_free_lunch.label: Free lunch (count)
frl_free_lunch.desc: Students eligible for free meals (raw count).

frl_reduced_lunch.label: Reduced lunch (count)
frl_reduced_lunch.desc: Students eligible for reduced-price meals (raw count).

pct_free_lunch.label: % Free lunch
pct_free_lunch.desc: Share of students on free meals (free ÷ 2022 enrollment); proxy for poverty.

pct_frl.label: % FRL
pct_frl.desc: Share of students eligible for free OR reduced-price meals; standard poverty proxy.

pct_direct_cert.label: % Direct cert
pct_direct_cert.desc: Share directly certified for free meals via SNAP/TANF/foster; proxy for deep poverty.

pct_asian.label: % Asian
pct_asian.desc: Share of students identifying as Asian, 2025-26.

pct_black.label: % Black
pct_black.desc: Share of students identifying as Black/African American, 2025-26.

pct_hispanic.label: % Hispanic
pct_hispanic.desc: Share of students identifying as Hispanic/Latino, 2025-26.

pct_white.label: % White
pct_white.desc: Share of students identifying as White, 2025-26.

pct_multiracial.label: % Multiracial
pct_multiracial.desc: Share identifying as two or more races, 2025-26.

pct_bipoc.label: % BIPOC
pct_bipoc.desc: Share of students identifying as any race/ethnicity other than White (1 − % White), 2025-26.

avg_prof_2425.label: Avg proficiency 24-25
avg_prof_2425.desc: Average of % ELA + % Math meeting/exceeding (2024-25). Single number for cross-school performance comparison.

service_load.label: Service load (weighted)
service_load.desc: Cost-weighted composite: 1.0×% deep poverty + 2.5×% SPED + 1.5×% English learners. Weights approximate per-pupil cost ratios from weighted-student-funding formulas (Boston, Hawaii). A higher index means heavier per-student support load.

support_staff_per_100.label: Support staff per 100
support_staff_per_100.desc: Counselor + social worker + psychologist FTE, per 100 students (denominator: 2020-21 CRDC enrollment). Reflects 2020-21 staffing; base allocations make smaller schools score higher per-pupil.

pct_chronic_absent_2021.label: % chronic absent (21)
pct_chronic_absent_2021.desc: Share of students who missed ≥10% of enrolled days in the 2020-21 school year. Post-COVID but still pandemic-era; treat as floor on current rates. Clamped at 100%; a few alternative schools' raw CRDC counts exceed point-in-time enrollment due to mid-year mobility.

suspensions_per_100_2021.label: OSS per 100
suspensions_per_100_2021.desc: Out-of-school suspension instances per 100 students (2020-21). Counts events not students; one student can be suspended multiple times.

counselors_fte_2021.label: Counselor FTE
counselors_fte_2021.desc: Full-time-equivalent school counselors as reported to CRDC for 2020-21.

social_workers_fte_2021.label: Social worker FTE
social_workers_fte_2021.desc: Full-time-equivalent school social workers as reported to CRDC for 2020-21.

teachers_fte_2023.label: Teacher FTE (2023)
teachers_fte_2023.desc: Classroom teacher full-time-equivalents as reported to NCES for fall 2023. Excludes support staff (counselors, social workers, nurses) and non-instructional roles.

students_per_teacher_2023.label: Students per teacher (2023)
students_per_teacher_2023.desc: Same-year ratio: fall 2023 NCES enrollment ÷ fall 2023 NCES teacher FTE. PPS in-scope median is ~17; small alternative and K-8 programs skew low, DLI and large elementaries skew high.

teachers_fte_2021.label: Teacher FTE (2021)
teachers_fte_2021.desc: Classroom teacher full-time-equivalents as reported to CRDC for 2020-21. COVID-era snapshot.

students_per_teacher_2021.label: Students per teacher (2021)
students_per_teacher_2021.desc: Same-year ratio: 2020-21 CRDC enrollment ÷ 2020-21 CRDC teacher FTE. COVID-era snapshot; shown alongside 2023 for trend context.

airflow_rooms_tested.label: Rooms airflow-tested
airflow_rooms_tested.desc: Count of classrooms and shared spaces airflow-tested by Amerseco/Neudorfer in the PPS 2021 NEBB-certified survey. Coverage is typically 20-40 rooms per elementary and 80-150 per high school.

airflow_ach_e_median.label: ACH_e median (2021)
airflow_ach_e_median.desc: Median ASHRAE Total Effective Air Changes per Hour without portable filtration — the rate at which a room's air is fully cycled through HVAC filtration and outside air dilution. Lancet COVID-era guidance recommends ≥6 ACH for classrooms; ≥3 ACH is a bare-minimum floor. Measured 2021; includes MERV-13 upgrades done by then.

airflow_pct_below_3_ach.label: % rooms < 3 ACH_e
airflow_pct_below_3_ach.desc: Share of tested rooms with effective ACH below 3 — the lower-bound floor in Lancet COVID ventilation guidance. Districtwide median is near 60%; buildings without filter upgrades are much higher.

airflow_pct_below_6_ach.label: % rooms < 6 ACH_e
airflow_pct_below_6_ach.desc: Share of tested rooms below the Lancet-recommended 6 ACH benchmark for classrooms. Most PPS rooms fall below this standard.

airflow_filter_upgraded.label: MERV-13 upgrade?
airflow_filter_upgraded.desc: Whether a MERV-13 filter upgrade had been installed in this building's HVAC as of the 2021 airflow survey. Filter upgrades raise effective ACH even without added outside air.

prof_residual.label: Proficiency residual
prof_residual.desc: Each school's avg proficiency minus what a linear fit on % BIPOC would predict. Positive = outperforms demographics; negative = underperforms.

affordable_units_within_1mi.label: Afford. units in catchment
affordable_units_within_1mi.desc: Total existing affordable housing units inside the school's PPS attendance area (or a 1-mile radius for schools without a published catchment).

pipeline_affordable_units_within_1mi.label: Pipeline afford. units
pipeline_affordable_units_within_1mi.desc: Affordable units in projects currently in development inside the school's PPS attendance area (2023–2027).

pipeline_family_units_within_1mi.label: Pipeline family units
pipeline_family_units_within_1mi.desc: 2+BR pipeline units inside the school's PPS attendance area; proxy for future families with kids.

n_pipeline_projects_within_1mi.label: Pipeline projects
n_pipeline_projects_within_1mi.desc: Number of affordable housing projects in development inside the school's PPS attendance area.

permits_units_within_1mi_since_2022.label: Permitted units (2022+)
permits_units_within_1mi_since_2022.desc: New residential units on building permits issued since 2022-01-01 inside the school's PPS attendance area (single-family, ADUs, and multifamily) (all tenures). Schools without a published catchment fall back to a 1-mile radius.

n_permits_within_1mi_since_2022.label: Permits (2022+)
n_permits_within_1mi_since_2022.desc: Number of residential building permits issued since 2022-01-01 inside the school's PPS attendance area. Permits = approved to build; not all reach completion.

bli_forecast_units_within_catchment.label: BLI forecast units (~2035)
bli_forecast_units_within_catchment.desc: Projected new residential units by ~2035 inside the school's PPS attendance area, from Metro's 2045 Building Land Inventory (BLI) Housing-Employment Allocation grid. Area-weighted sum over intersecting BLI grid cells; schools without a catchment fall back to a 1-mile buffer around the school's point location. A forward-looking counterpart to the (backwards-looking) permits metric.

nearest_alt_school_mi.label: Miles to nearest alt.
nearest_alt_school_mi.desc: Great-circle distance from this low-enrollment school to the nearest larger same-grade-band school (elementary, k8, middle, or alternative). A rough proxy for transportation impact if this school were closed; actual PPS reassignment may differ.

nearest_alt_school_name.label: Nearest alt. school
nearest_alt_school_name.desc: Name of the closest larger school of the same grade band.

programs.label: Programs
programs.desc: Specialized programs hosted at this school. Captures Dual Language Immersion (DLI) language tracks plus K-8 focus options (Arts, Environmental, STEAM, Creative Science, TAG, Alternative). 'Access to programs' is one of the four PPS-announced closure-decision factors; closing a DLI host school would cut off that language pathway in its catchment.
programs.source: PPS Dual Language + Enrollment & Transfer pages

has_dli.label: Hosts DLI?
has_dli.desc: School hosts a Dual Language Immersion program in any language.
has_dli.source: Derived from PPS DLI program list

dli_languages.label: DLI languages
dli_languages.desc: Dual Language Immersion language(s) hosted at this school (Spanish, Chinese, Japanese, Russian, Vietnamese).

has_focus_option.label: Hosts focus option?
has_focus_option.desc: School is one of the K-8 focus-option / specialized-curriculum programs (Buckman Arts, Bridger Creative Science, Sunnyside Environmental, Winterhaven STEAM, Odyssey TAG, ACCESS, MLC).

dli_students_2526.label: DLI students 25-26
dli_students_2526.desc: Students enrolled in this school's Dual Language Immersion strand(s), October 2025. Populated only for the ~32 schools that host a DLI program. A school's total enrollment is divided between neighborhood/mainstream students and DLI-strand students; ODE's Fall Membership cannot separate them.

neighborhood_students_2526.label: Neighborhood students 25-26
neighborhood_students_2526.desc: Total enrollment minus DLI-strand enrollment. At a DLI host school this is the mainstream/English-medium population; the cohort that would remain if the DLI program were relocated.

pct_dli_2526.label: % DLI 25-26
pct_dli_2526.desc: Share of this school's students in a DLI strand. Ranges from ~4% at schools where a single DLI cohort visits (Jefferson) to 100% at whole-school DLI programs (Lent, Rigler, Richmond).

enrollment_pct_change_7yr.label: Enrollment Δ% 2018→2025
enrollment_pct_change_7yr.desc: 7-year enrollment change from fall 2018 (NCES CCD) to fall 2025 (ODE). The in-scope set lost 20% over this window (median school -19%); schools well below that baseline are losing students faster than PPS as a whole.

prc_baseline_2024_25.label: PRC baseline 24-25
prc_baseline_2024_25.desc: PRC's own historic 2024-25 enrollment used as the base year for its 10-year forecast (Table 5.5, medium scenario). Usually matches ODE 2024-25, but differs at co-located programs — e.g. Hayhurst's PRC baseline is Hayhurst-proper only and excludes Odyssey K-8.

enrollment_forecast_2025_26.label: Forecast 25-26 (med.)
enrollment_forecast_2025_26.desc: PRC medium-scenario enrollment forecast for 2025-26 (per-school, all programs combined).

enrollment_forecast_2030_31.label: Forecast 30-31 (med.)
enrollment_forecast_2030_31.desc: PRC medium-scenario enrollment forecast for 2030-31 (5-year horizon).

enrollment_forecast_2034_35.label: Forecast 34-35 (med.)
enrollment_forecast_2034_35.desc: PRC medium-scenario enrollment forecast for 2034-35 (10-year horizon). The closure argument hinges on future utilization, not current — this is the per-school number PRC published.

enrollment_forecast_2034_35_low.label: Forecast 34-35 (low)
enrollment_forecast_2034_35_low.desc: Low-scenario 2034-35 forecast. PRC only publishes Low/High at the district-and-grade level, so each school's band is the medium forecast × the district-wide Low/Medium ratio at its grade band (K-5 elementaries: ×0.869; 6-8 middle: ×0.859).
enrollment_forecast_2034_35_low.source: Derived: PRC Tables 5.3/5.5 (low × med ratio by grade band)

enrollment_forecast_2034_35_high.label: Forecast 34-35 (high)
enrollment_forecast_2034_35_high.desc: High-scenario 2034-35 forecast. PRC only publishes Low/High at the district-and-grade level, so each school's band is the medium forecast × the district-wide High/Medium ratio at its grade band (K-5 elementaries: ×1.221; 6-8 middle: ×1.075).
enrollment_forecast_2034_35_high.source: Derived: PRC Tables 5.4/5.5 (high × med ratio by grade band)

enrollment_forecast_pct_change_10yr.label: Forecast Δ% 10yr (24-25→34-35)
enrollment_forecast_pct_change_10yr.desc: Projected 10-year enrollment change from PRC's 2024-25 baseline to its 2034-35 medium-scenario forecast. Uses PRC's own historic baseline (not ODE) so co-located programs like Odyssey/Hayhurst net out correctly.

utilization_pct_2034_35.label: Forecast utilization 34-35
utilization_pct_2034_35.desc: Projected 2034-35 enrollment (PRC medium) ÷ 2021 functional capacity. A forward-looking counterpart to current utilization. Below 50% signals deeply underused by PPS's own planning standard — even if today's utilization is higher.

scatters.title: Enrollment vs. building utilization
scatters.subtitle: Utilization = current enrollment ÷ 2021 functional capacity. The closure shortlist (low-enrollment schools) clusters in the lower-left quadrant of underused buildings. Horizontal line at 100% marks PPS's own planning capacity.

scatters.title: Math proficiency vs. % low-income
scatters.subtitle: Classic income-achievement gradient. Schools above the trend line outperform expectations; below, underperform.

scatters.title: Avg proficiency vs. deep poverty (direct certification)
scatters.subtitle: Strongest correlation in the dataset (r = -0.90). Direct certification (SNAP/TANF/foster) predicts test scores more tightly than free/reduced lunch or any race/ethnicity variable.

scatters.title: Enrollment vs. % BIPOC students
scatters.subtitle: Across PPS, smaller-enrollment schools do not serve a disproportionately BIPOC student body; the cloud is roughly flat.

scatters.title: Chronic absenteeism vs. avg proficiency (2020-21)
scatters.subtitle: Strong inverse relationship: schools where 1 in 3+ students were chronically absent post-COVID rarely exceed 30% proficiency now.

scatters.title: Student-support staff vs. deep poverty
scatters.subtitle: Counselor + social worker + psychologist FTE per 100 students (CRDC 2020-21). Base allocations mean small schools score higher per-pupil by default; read with enrollment in mind. A high-poverty school well below the cloud has less per-pupil support than its peers. Alliance High School excluded as a staffing outlier.

scatters.title: 7-year enrollment change vs. current size (2018 → 2025)
scatters.subtitle: Long-term sustainability check. The in-scope set as a whole shrank ~20% over seven years; schools well below that line are losing students faster than the district overall.

scatters.title: Projected 10-year enrollment change vs. current size
scatters.subtitle: PSU's Population Research Center projects districtwide enrollment to fall from ~43k to ~37k by 2034-35 (medium scenario). Schools in the lower-left — already small today and projected to keep shrinking — are the ones where future-utilization pressure is most acute.

scatters.title: Current vs. projected 2034-35 utilization
scatters.subtitle: How underused each building gets by the end of PRC's 10-year forecast. The 45° line means 'holding steady'; below it means the building empties further. Below 50% is PPS's own threshold for 'substantially underused'.

scatters.title: Student/teacher ratio vs. enrollment
scatters.subtitle: Smaller schools sit lower because teacher FTE has a floor (a K-5 still needs one teacher per grade level regardless of section size). Schools well above the trend are stretched thin for their size; schools well below are staffed unusually richly. Ratio uses fall 2023 NCES; enrollment is fall 2025 ODE for consistency with other scatters.

0.name: High-need urban
0.desc: Mid-poverty, BIPOC-majority elementaries, mostly stable enrollment, lower test scores.

1.name: Large + growing
1.desc: Larger schools with strong nearby housing pipeline; no closure candidates.

3.name: Aging, high-SPED
3.desc: Tiny cluster: very old buildings, very high SPED %, declining enrollment.
